core_mechanism: a compression/density-driven structural phase transition between competing ordered lattice morphologies, in which many pairwise-interacting particle-clusters reorganize from an isotropic close-packed (bcc) lattice into an anisotropic rod/lamellar lattice by pairwise coalescence of neighbors through an intermediate ordered (zigzag) configuration
brief_reason: The abstract shows that increasing compression drives a bcc lattice of spherical clusters to reorganize into a lattice of rod-like structures via a zigzag intermediate formed by fusion of two neighboring clusters, i.e. a control-parameter-driven transition between ordered morphologies mediated by coalescence (demonstrated here with a molecular-dynamics simulation).
